name: Housing Resources INC - Waukesha
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing HUD-approved homebuyer education, mortgage assistance, and financial empowerment services across seven Wisconsin counties since 1991.
description_long:
Housing Resources Inc (HRI) was founded in 1991 in the Milwaukee area with a mission to provide homebuyer education and counseling services. Over three decades, the organization has established itself as a dedicated non-profit serving low and moderate-income families across Milwaukee, Waukesha, Ozaukee, Washington, Racine, Kenosha, and Walworth counties. The organization's core philosophy centers on preparing people for successful homeownership and fostering broader financial empowerment through education and advocacy.

HRI offers comprehensive services for both prospective and current homeowners. For home buyers, they provide HUD-approved homebuyer education classes covering topics like credit management, mortgage products, affordability assessment, and home shopping fundamentals. Their one-on-one pre-purchase counseling guides clients through financial assessment, loan selection, credit correction, and the entire closing process. For homeowners, HRI offers post-purchase counseling, default prevention assistance, reverse mortgage counseling, tool loan programs for maintenance, and minor home repair support. The organization also connects clients with multiple down payment assistance programs including Downpayment Plus, Milwaukee Home Down Payment Program, and employer-assisted housing initiatives.

What distinguishes HRI is their integrated approach combining education, counseling, and direct mortgage assistance under one non-profit structure. They operate the Racine Financial Empowerment Center, coordinate with established partners, and administer county-specific mortgage assistance programs (Milwaukee, Racine, and Waukesha). Their ability to help clients access multiple grant programs and their long operational history since 1991 demonstrates stability and deep community integration. The organization emphasizes accessibility through both in-person workshops and virtual homebuyer education options.

HRI operates transparently as a 501(c)(3) non-profit with no financial products to sell, making their counseling genuinely client-centered rather than profit-driven. However, their services are geographically limited to specific Wisconsin counties, and the website does not specify wait times, availability windows, or detailed eligibility criteria. While they address foreclosure prevention and mortgage assistance, the scope and income limits of these programs are not detailed on the public website.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling services with certified specialists providing one-on-one guidance
Free or low-cost homebuyer education workshops covering credit, affordability, loan products, and financial management
Access to multiple down payment assistance programs including employer-assisted housing and city/county-specific grants
Integrated services for both home buyers and homeowners, including default counseling and reverse mortgage education
Established non-profit (since 1991) with demonstrated track record across seven Wisconsin counties
Post-purchase counseling and mortgage assistance programs to help families avoid foreclosure
Virtual homebuyer education options alongside in-person classes and workshops
cons: Services limited to Milwaukee, Waukesha, Ozaukee, Washington, Racine, Kenosha, and Walworth counties only
Website does not specify current wait times, application processing timelines, or detailed program eligibility requirements
Scope and income limits for mortgage assistance programs not clearly detailed on public website
No information provided about counselor certifications beyond HUD approval or staff qualifications
Tool loan program and minor home repair services lack specifics on cost, availability, or repair scope limitations
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Wisconsin counties served seeking free pre-purchase counseling and down payment assistance
Low to moderate-income families needing credit education, budgeting help, and mortgage product guidance
Current homeowners facing financial hardship or mortgage default looking for counseling and assistance programs
Individuals seeking employer-assisted housing programs or access to multiple down payment grant opportunities
diagnosis: HRI is best for first-time and low-to-moderate-income homebuyers in their seven-county Wisconsin service area seeking free, non-profit counseling and down payment assistance. Primary caveat: services are geographically restricted, and specific program eligibility, wait times, and detailed scope are not transparent on their public website.
services: HUD-approved homebuyer education classes (online and monthly workshops)
One-on-one pre-purchase counseling with financial assessment and loan guidance
Down payment assistance grant application support (multiple programs)
Credit report services (pay for credit report option mentioned)
Tool loan program for home maintenance
Mortgage assistance programs (Milwaukee County, City of Racine, Waukesha County)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Nonprofit housing counselor providing HUD-approved homebuyer education and mortgage assistance across seven Wisconsin counties.
